acceptance_targets: [ch16-award-01] In the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:471, each block must either reveal a genuinely new motive or materially change the power balance; if all three passages still function mainly as direct explanation of June's grievance architecture, fail.

[ch16-craft-01] After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:353, every subsequent exchange through the severance terms at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:509 must either alter Lena's next move or introduce a cost that was not already on the table; if a later confession block can be removed without changing Lena's debt/boundary terms, fail.

[ch16-prose-01] In the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:395, the same beats must be rendered in complete sentences; if any cited narrative sentence still depends on subject-dropping or article-stripping for punch, fail.

[MEDIUM_CH16_DEFAULT_FACIAL_TIC] Pass if a reader moving through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7295-7740 can track the escalation through varied, character-specific behavior and finds no more than one 'still/flat/blank/flatter' construction in that span. Fail if multiple key turns still default to the same facial shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:471, each block must either reveal a genuinely new motive or materially change the power balance; if all three passages still function mainly as direct explanation of June's grievance architecture, fail.
findings.problem: After June's authorship and money exploitation are already established, the chapter keeps converting live danger into explicit motive-summary. The confrontation inventories jealousy, class resentment, possession, and desire so completely that it sheds some of the negative space and residue an award-level scene needs.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory blocks beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:283, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:379, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:471 so at least one core motive is carried by deflection, loaded omission, or behavior rather than a clean self-diagnostic paragraph. Keep jealousy, opportunism, and the thrill of precision legible, but do not let June explain all three at full essay clarity.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:353, every subsequent exchange through the severance terms at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:509 must either alter Lena's next move or introduce a cost that was not already on the table; if a later confession block can be removed without changing Lena's debt/boundary terms, fail.
findings.problem: The scene reaches its sharpest irreversible admission when June answers 'Sometimes' to sexual gratification, but the chapter then runs two more confession loops before the debt and severance terms land. That post-peak stretch restates adjacent injuries rather than escalating through new consequence, so the confrontation plateaus instead of tightening.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or restructure the post-admission material beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:369 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:461. After the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:353, any remaining exchange should trigger a new consequence, interruption, or concrete choice, not another paraphrase of why June did it.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:395, the same beats must be rendered in complete sentences; if any cited narrative sentence still depends on subject-dropping or article-stripping for punch, fail.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly falls into clipped, subject-dropped fragments such as 'Not long,' 'Not complete,' and 'Not tears.' Used this often, the move becomes a visible tic and directly violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration. In a confrontation chapter that depends on sentence control, that mannerism is costly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the clipped narration around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:395 into grammatically complete sentences that preserve pressure without relying on omitted subjects or verbs.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader moving through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7295-7740 can track the escalation through varied, character-specific behavior and finds no more than one 'still/flat/blank/flatter' construction in that span. Fail if multiple key turns still default to the same facial shorthand.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern: the default facial/stasis shorthand 'went still/flat/blank/flatter' appears across at least five chapters, and this confrontation chapter uses several versions at 7295, 7561, 7657, and 7739. Here the repetition is most damaging because the showdown should feel scalpel-precise; instead, the stock shorthand starts flattening the very scene that needs the freshest physical writing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Across snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7295, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7561, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7657, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7739, replace the repeated 'still/flat/blank/flatter' beats with differentiated physical signals of pressure: what June does with the cigarette, lighter, sink, or posture; how Lena's body arrests, advances, or recoils. Keep at most one generic facial-flatness beat in the whole confrontation.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
